---
output:
  md_document:
    variant: gfm
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

# ggpubr

[![License: GPL v2+](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-GPL%20v2+-blue.svg)](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-2.0)

`ggpubr` provides publication-ready plotting helpers built on top of `ggplot2`.

## Installation

```r
# CRAN
install.packages("ggpubr")

# Development version
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("kassambara/ggpubr")
```

## Highlights in this branch

- Modern compatibility updates for current `ggplot2`, `dplyr`, and `tidyr`.
- New p-value formatting helpers:
  - `format_p_value()`
  - `get_p_format_style()`
  - `list_p_format_styles()`
- Extended p-value formatting parameters across statistical layers:
  - `p.format.style`
  - `p.digits`
  - `p.leading.zero`
  - `p.min.threshold`
  - `p.decimal.mark`

## Example

```r
library(ggpubr)
library(ggplot2)

p <- ggboxplot(ToothGrowth, x = "supp", y = "len", color = "supp")
p + stat_compare_means(p.format.style = "apa")
```

## Documentation

- Package site: <https://rpkgs.datanovia.com/ggpubr/>
- Source code: <https://github.com/kassambara/ggpubr>
- Issues: <https://github.com/kassambara/ggpubr/issues>

## Citation

```r
citation("ggpubr")
```

## Credits

- Original author and maintainer: Alboukadel Kassambara
- Contributor: Laszlo Erdey (Faculty of Economics and Business, University of Debrecen, Hungary)
